data.description: The live link-direction round-trip test asserted the WRONG expected relation. Its fallback expected_relation = 'blocks' if b_is_inward else 'depends_on' was built on an inverted assumption that set_relationship(A,B,'Blocks') ('A blocks B') records B as inwardIssue on A. Verified against LIVE Jira (navasage) in BOTH directions: 'A blocks B' records B OUTWARD on A and resolve_inbound_link/map_remote_links correctly reconstruct 'blocks'; 'A depends_on B' (swap) records B INWARD and reconstructs 'depends_on'. The differ direction handling is correct (faithful round-trip); the TEST expectation was wrong and failed against live. Fix: assert round-trip fidelity (an applied 'blocks' dep reconstructs 'blocks', B outward on A). Pre-existing (predates the epic; eefd did not touch the inbound direction path). Discovered during epic bbf1 live validation.
data.title: test_link_sync_roundtrip_live inverts blocks/depends_on direction expectation
